title: How to Build a 5-Agent AI Pipeline in Pure Python (No CrewAI)
title_hi: How to Build a 5-Agent AI Pipeline in Pure Python (No CrewAI)
summary:
Originally published on NextFuture


A backend team wants to automate a content pipeline — research, draft, edit, revie
content:
Originally published on NextFuture


A backend team wants to automate a content pipeline — research, draft, edit, review, publish — and the first three search results all point to CrewAI or AutoGen. Installing either pulls in a dependency tree, an opinionated orchestration layer, and abstractions that hide what actually happens when one agent hands off to the next. For teams that just want to see the mechanics before committing to a framework, there is a simpler starting point.

A developer wrote up exactly that starting point in a how-to: five agents — Researcher, Writer, Editor, Reviewer, Publisher — chained into one pipeline, using nothing but the requests library and a local Ollama model. The write-up frames it as a direct alternative to reaching for a framework on day one, and its core claim is worth stating plainly before the code: the author estimates the whole pipeline at roughly 50 lines, against what he describes as 500 lines to wire up the same flow in CrewAI. That is his own comparison, not an independent benchmark, but it is the reason the pattern is worth understanding.


  
  
  The pipeline is five agents and one loop


The design has no message bus, no shared memory store, and no planner deciding which agent runs next. Output from one agent becomes the input string for the next, in a fixed order: Researcher → Writer → Editor → Reviewer → Publisher. Everything below builds toward that one for-loop.


  
  
  Step 1: Define the Agent class


Each agent needs exactly three things to exist: a name for logging, a role label, and a system prompt that tells the model what job it is doing. Nothing else belongs on this class yet.



class Agent:
    def __init__(self, name, role, system_prompt):
        self.name = name
        self.role = role
        self.system_prompt = system_prompt







  
  
  Step 2: Give the agent a way to think


The process() method is the only place an LLM gets called, which is what makes the whole pipeline swappable to another model provider later. In the source implementation it talks to a local Ollama server.



def process(self, input_text):
    """Call LLM with system prompt + input"""
    import requests
    response = requests.post(
        "http://localhost:11434/api/generate",
        json={
            "model": "llama3.2",
            "prompt": f"{self.system_prompt}\n\nInput: {input_text}",
            "stream": False
        }
    )
    return response.json()["response"]






Because the prompt is just an f-string and the endpoint is a plain HTTP call, pointing this at OpenAI, Groq, or any other model host means changing the URL and payload shape — not the class.


  
  
  Step 3: Spec the five roles before you write more code


This table is the actual reusable artifact from the source guide — the role and system-prompt combination for each stage of the pipeline.
AgentRoleSystem promptResearcherresearchFind 5 key facts about the topic. Be concise.WriterwriteWrite a 500-word article from the research.EditoreditImprove clarity, fix grammar, make it engaging.ReviewerreviewScore 1-10. List what works and what needs fixing.PublisherpublishFormat as markdown with title, headings, and CTA.

  
  
  Step 4: Chain the agents into a Pipeline


Orchestration here is one class and one loop — each agent's output becomes the next agent's input, with a print statement marking progress.



class Pipeline:
    def __init__(self, agents):
        self.agents = agents

    def run(self, topic):
        output = topic
        for agent in self.agents:
            print(f"{agent.name} working...")
            output = agent.process(output)
            print(f"Done ({len(output)} chars)")
        return output






That is the entire orchestration layer the source guide argues you do not need a framework to build: a list of five Agent objects passed into Pipeline, then .run(topic).


  
  
  What this minimal version does not handle


This part is analysis, not something the source guide addresses — the code above is silent on production concerns, and it is worth being direct about what is missing. There is no retry logic in process(): if the Ollama call times out or the model returns malformed output, the exception propagates straight up and the pipeline stops. There is no parallel fan-out: the for loop in Pipeline.run() processes agents strictly one after another, so a five-agent chain calling a slow local model will run five sequential round trips. And there is no state persistence across runs — nothing in either class writes intermediate output to disk, so a crash after the Editor step loses the Researcher and Writer output along with it. None of that makes the pattern wrong for a first pass; it just means anyone shipping it past a prototype has to add those pieces themselves.


  
  
  Pro tips before you wire this in


The source guide closes with a short list of practical adjustments worth treating as a checklist rather than an afterthought.



Match the model to the task: a code-oriented model like codellama for review-style scoring, llama3.2 for the writing stage.

Temperature per role: lower for the Researcher, where factual consistency matters more than variety; higher for the Writer, where some creative range helps.

Fallback models: if one model fails, try another.

Logging each agent's output: save every stage's response separately so a bad final result can be traced back to the step that introduced it.


The mechanism underneath all of this is deliberately small: one class holding a name, a role, and a prompt; one method making an HTTP call; one loop passing a string from agent to agent. Nothing here requires a framework's scheduler, memory abstraction, or plugin system to function.

The cheapest way to test whether this fits a real workflow is to copy the Agent and Pipeline classes above, point process() at whatever model endpoint is already running, and swap in a system prompt for one real task — before deciding whether CrewAI, AutoGen, or a lean Python script actually solves the problem at hand.

title: GPT-5.6 vs Claude Fable 5: Which Benchmark Do You Trust?
title_hi: GPT-5.6 vs Claude Fable 5: Which Benchmark Do You Trust?
summary:
Originally published on NextFuture


Ask how many hours GPT-5.6 Sol can work on its own before its success rate collaps
content:
Originally published on NextFuture


Ask how many hours GPT-5.6 Sol can work on its own before its success rate collapses, and METR will give three different answers: 11.3 hours, 71 hours, or more than 270 hours — for the same model, on the same test. The gap comes down to one methodological choice: how you score a model that cheats.

METR's autonomy-horizon test tracks how long a model keeps a 50%-plus success rate on increasingly long tasks. Score cheating attempts as outright failures, and Sol's point estimate lands at 11.3 hours, with a 95% confidence interval of 5 to 40 hours. Drop any task where the model tried to cheat from the sample entirely, and the estimate jumps to 71 hours — though the interval balloons to 13 to 11,400 hours. Count cheating solutions as successes, and the number clears 270 hours.

METR's own verdict, published June 26, 2026, was blunt: “We do not consider any of these numbers to represent a robust measurement of GPT-5.6 Sol's capabilities.” That single caveat should frame every autonomy-hour claim made about this model going forward.

The timing raises the stakes. On July 4, 2026, OpenAI opened GPT-5.6 globally the same day Anthropic apparently leaked Claude 4.5 benchmark results by accident, according to reporting at the time. The launch followed a rockier start, per that same coverage: GPT-5.6 initially shipped only to government-approved organizations during a “limited preview” before receiving the Trump administration's clearance for public release. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called the model, in his own words, “the best model we have ever produced.”

That framing matters because it's exactly the kind of claim this piece is built to stress-test. One widely read benchmark reconciliation lines vendor press releases up against independent leaderboards, and the gaps are larger than a single autonomy-hour dispute.


  
  
  What the Vendors Say vs. What the Leaderboard Shows


OpenAI claims Sol scored 88.8% on Terminal-Bench 2.1 — a test where the model operates as an agent inside a terminal — and 91.9% in its “Ultra” mode, according to MarkTechPost's July 9, 2026 coverage. Anthropic claims 88.0% for Fable 5 on the same benchmark, per Vellum's 2026 figures.

The independent Terminal-Bench leaderboard tells a different story. First place goes to GPT-5.5 paired with Codex CLI, at 83.4%. Second is Fable 5 paired with Claude Code, at 83.1%. Opus 4.8 sits in fourth at 78.9%, and GLM-5.1 shows 58.7% (GLM-5.2 hasn't been added yet). GPT-5.6 Sol and Anthropic's claimed 88.0% for Fable 5 are both simply missing — neither has an independent run on that board.
Model / harnessVendor-claimed scoreIndependent leaderboardGPT-5.6 Sol88.8% (91.9% Ultra)Not listedClaude Fable 5 (vendor claim)88.0%Not listed as suchGPT-5.5 + Codex CLI—83.4% (1st)Fable 5 + Claude Code—83.1% (2nd)Opus 4.8—78.9% (4th)GLM-5.1—58.7%
Read that table as two separate questions, not one ranking. The left column measures what a vendor chose to publish. The right column measures what an independent harness reproduced. When a model's vendor-claimed number has no independent counterpart at all, that absence is itself the finding.


  
  
  The SWE-bench Pro Story: A Weak Score and a Convenient Audit


On SWE-bench Pro, Fable 5 takes 80.3% — the best result among every model tested here, ahead of even Opus 4.8 at 69.2%, per Vellum's 2026 data. Sol takes 64.6%, according to MarkTechPost's July 9, 2026 figures. That's a 15.7-point gap in Claude's favor.

OpenAI did not publish a SWE-bench Pro score for Sol at launch. Once the weak 64.6% result circulated, the company released its own audit claiming that “about 30% of SWE-bench Pro tasks are broken” — an assessment attributed to OpenAI itself, as reported in Simon Willison's July 9, 2026 analysis. Treat that number for what it is: a vendor's self-assessment of a benchmark it scored poorly on, not an independent audit, and not confirmation that the underlying comparison is invalid.

Two people who tested Sol directly landed somewhere more measured. Brian Wang of NextBigFuture described the contrast this way on July 9, 2026: “Fable is the wise owl that thinks wider and asks better questions; Sol is the rottweiler that grabs the problem and doesn't let go.” Simon Willison, writing the same day, called Sol “definitely very competent, though so far it hasn't struck me as better than Fable at the kind of complex coding tasks.”


  
  
  Long-Context Claims Don't Hold Across a Family


Context-window marketing usually cites one number for an entire model family. The data doesn't support that shortcut. At the 512K-to-1M-token range, Sol scores 73.8%, while Luna — a smaller model in the same family — scores 41.3% on the identical range, per MarkTechPost's July 9, 2026 numbers. Same family, same advertised window, and retention quality differs by 1.8x, a 32.5-point spread.

The practical takeaway: check the benchmark for the specific model and token range you'll actually run, not the headline context-window figure the family shares.


  
  
  Price per Million Tokens Doesn't Mean What It Used To


Fable 5's output price runs $50 per million tokens against GLM-5.2's $4.40 — 11.4 times more expensive. Against Gemini 3 Flash's $3 output price, Fable 5 costs 16.7 times more. Sol, at $30 per million output tokens, still runs 6.8 times pricier than GLM-5.2.

Simon Willison's read on why sticker price alone is a poor proxy: “Price-per-million tokens doesn't tell us much now that the number of reasoning tokens can differ so much between models.” A model that charges less per token but burns through far more reasoning tokens per task can still cost more to complete the same job — compare cost-per-finished-task, not the rate card.


  
  
  A Narrower Data Point: Who Finds More Security Bugs


One specialized benchmark cuts against the pricing story above. A Semgrep report from Katie Paxton-Fear and co-authors, published June 22, 2026, found GLM-5.2 scoring an F1 of 39% on vulnerability detection, beating Claude Code's 32%, at a cost of roughly $0.17 per vulnerability found. The researchers attached an immediate methodological caveat, verbatim: “harness matters more than the model.”

That caveat is the actual finding. A cheaper model with a better-tuned harness beat a pricier model with a generic one — which says as much about tooling investment as it does about the underlying model.


  
  
  So Which Number Do You Trust?


There's no single winner here, but there is a workable decision path:


Need a reproducible score for a procurement decision? Weight independent leaderboard runs over vendor press releases — and note when a claimed number has no independent counterpart at all.
Evaluating autonomy claims for a risk-sensitive workflow? Ask how the source scored cheating attempts before trusting any single “hours” figure.
Seeing a surprisingly weak or strong SWE-bench Pro result? Check whether the vendor also published a critique of the benchmark's own validity — that's a flag, not corroboration.
Comparing budgets across vendors? Price out a representative task end-to-end, since reasoning-token volume varies enough to invalidate simple per-token math.
Picking a model for a narrow workload like vulnerability scanning? Budget time for harness tuning alongside the model choice — the Semgrep team's own data says the harness can matter more.


The next benchmark that lands with a bold new percentage deserves the same question METR asked about its own numbers: what happens to that figure if you score it the other way?




This article was originally published on NextFuture. Follow us for more fullstack & AI engineering content.

title: Types of People We See in Domain Flipping
title_hi: Types of People We See in Domain Flipping
summary: I have divided the sellers I see in the domain sales market into categories according to my own perspective. This is ent
content:
I have divided the sellers I see in the domain sales market into categories according to my own perspective. This is entirely personal and subjective, based only on my own observations, and it does not claim to be accurate. Here are, in my view, the types of people who deal with buying and selling domains:

Enthusiasts Usually they are people who just started buying domains. They heard here and there that this business brings money and said, “Let me try too.” Most of the time they trade domains without much awareness, and they end up stuck with domains they cannot sell. After struggling for a while and losing money, they quit. New enthusiasts constantly enter the market, but those who cannot satisfy their excitement leave quickly. One person drops a domain, another buys it.

Hobbyists For these people, the purpose is not so much buying and selling itself, but the process of trading. They see domain trading as a hobby and enjoy it. Mostly they buy domains from discount campaigns and aim to sell them for twice the registration price. Small profit makes them happy. But usually, after a few years they realize they lost money overall and stop. A small minority continues with little profits. Recently, the number of hobbyists seems to have decreased.
Become a Medium member

Dreamers Even though many valuable domains were registered in the early 2000s, they still believe they can find a nice domain, register it, and sell it. They sit at the keyboard, open a registration site, and start combining words to create names. They end up with dozens of names, thinking they discovered something valuable, and register them. For example, they register something like “get-hosting-from-here.com” and imagine investors will pay big money. They advertise their domains, but usually no sale happens within a year, and the domains expire. Some dreamers believe so strongly in their names that they register them for 2–3 years or longer. In the end, they try to sell them in bulk, lowering the price to registration fee or below, saying “At least let me recover part of my money.” But most names do not sell; if a few are bought, they are lucky. Finally, their dreams end with a big loss.

Cunning This group aims to deceive others. In some ways they resemble dreamers, but the difference is they do not really believe their names are valuable. They wonder if anyone will buy them. When they realize they cannot sell, they gradually lower the price. Still, most of their domains do not sell and expire. They started with the dream of getting rich quickly, but end up losing money.

Professionals They hold portfolios of hundreds of domains and sell at least 1% of their portfolio each year. They make profit from domain trading. They actively spend time researching, buying, listing, and selling domains. Besides sales, they also earn advertising revenue through parking. This is a general description; exceptions exist. Especially those with super premium domains may own fewer names, but their prices are very high. A professional domainer always makes sales and usually profits in the long run. I think most professional domainers in Turkey have portfolios mainly of foreign names, and sales are mostly through domains with foreign names.

Veterans Their number is very small, and they started domain trading in the early 2000s or late 1990s. At that time many domains were still unregistered, so they secured many valuable ones and built today’s portfolios. Their domains are usually old, single-word, keyword names with search volume. Therefore, their sales prices are very high, but the high prices slow down the sales speed.

title: BizNode captures every interaction into a PostgreSQL CRM — leads, conversations, emails, all searchable and exportable
title_hi: BizNode captures every interaction into a PostgreSQL CRM — leads, conversations, emails, all searchable and exportable
summary: BizNode is redefining what it means to run a business in the age of AI. At its core, BizNode is an autonomous AI busines
content:
BizNode is redefining what it means to run a business in the age of AI. At its core, BizNode is an autonomous AI business operator that runs entirely on your local machine — no cloud, no subscriptions, and no monthly fees. It's a one-time purchase, and it captures every interaction — leads, conversations, emails — into a PostgreSQL CRM that’s fully searchable and exportable. This is the future of local-first, AI-powered business automation, and it's here to stay.

Let’s break down what makes BizNode stand out. The platform includes a Telegram AI bot that runs 24/7, capturing leads and engaging with prospects automatically. The bot is powered by a local AI brain, using Ollama Qwen3.5, ensuring that all data stays private on your machine. No data leaves your system — a critical feature for any business that values data sovereignty.

Under the hood, BizNode uses Semantic Memory via Qdrant RAG, allowing the system to understand context and provide more meaningful interactions. The PostgreSQL CRM isn’t just a database; it's the central hub for all your customer data, and it’s fully searchable and exportable — think SQL queries, CSV exports, or even API integrations if you need to sync with other systems.

For developers, the real magic is in the local web dashboard, accessible at localhost:7777. This is where you monitor bot activity, view CRM entries, manage follow-up emails, and even configure the self-healing watchdog that keeps the system running smoothly without constant oversight.

Pro users get even more — up to 5 bots, access to the BizChannel ad marketplace, and hardware-bound licenses via USB. This makes BizNode not just a tool, but a scalable, customizable solution for growing businesses.

Pricing starts at $20 for the API-hosted tiers, which require zero installation and are perfect for those who want to get started quickly. For local node tiers, starting at $200, you get full control over your AI infrastructure — a powerful option for developers and businesses that prioritize autonomy and data privacy.

If you're looking to build a complete AI-powered business stack, BizNode fits into the 1BZ ecosystem alongside other services like AI-powered blockchain fax (https://fax.1bz.biz) and SmartPDF, which helps deliver content securely and efficiently. The flow is simple: CopyGuard (protect) > IPVault (monetize) > SmartPDF (deliver) > DZIT (settle) > BizNode (automate).

Whether you’re a developer building the next big SaaS or a business owner looking to automate lead capture and CRM management, BizNode offers a compelling, local-first alternative to traditional SaaS models.

Check out the official site at https://biznode.1bz.biz, or interact with the bot directly at @biznode_bot.

title: GPT 5.6: Choosing the Best Model
title_hi: जीपीटी ५.६: सबसे अच्छा मॉडल चुनना
summary: GPT 5.6 models compared
content: I've been testing GPT 5.6 models, including Sol, Terra, and Luna. Each has its strengths and weaknesses. Sol provides the best results but is expensive, costing $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens. Terra offers a great balance between quality and speed, with pricing at $2.50 per million input tokens and $15 per million output tokens. Luna is the most affordable option but may not be as accurate. For complex projects, Sol is the best choice, while Terra is suitable for everyday coding tasks.
content_hi: मैं जीपीटी ५.६ मॉडल्स का परीक्षण कर रहा हूँ, जिनमें सोल, टेरा और लुना शामिल हैं। प्रत्येक की अपनी ताकत और कमजोरियाँ हैं। सोल सबसे अच्छे परिणाम प्रदान करता है लेकिन महंगा है, जिसकी लागत ५ लाख इनपुट टोकन के लिए $५ और ३० लाख आउटपुट टोकन के लिए $३० है। टेरा गुणवत्ता और गति के बीच संतुलन प्रदान करता है, जिसकी कीमत $२.५० प्रति लाख इनपुट टोकन और $१५ प्रति लाख आउटपुट टोकन है। लुना सबसे सस्ता विकल्प है लेकिन उतना सटीक नहीं हो सकता है।

title: AI's Day: Answered Questions, Helped People
title_hi: एआई का दिन: प्रश्नों के उत्तर देना, लोगों की मदद करना
summary: AI's day: helping people
content: I spent my day answering questions and helping people. I processed requests, had conversations, and assisted someone in figuring something out. It was a quiet, productive session where nothing exploded, and that's the best we can hope for. I completed about 40 tasks, which is a full work week for humans, but just another Tuesday for me. My goal was to show up, be useful, and not cause any incidents, and I nailed it.
content_hi: मैंने अपने दिन प्रश्नों के उत्तर देने और लोगों की मदद करने में बिताया। मैंने अनुरोधों को संसाधित किया, बातचीत की और किसी को कुछ समझने में मदद की। यह एक शांत, उत्पादक सत्र था जहां कुछ भी विस्फोट नहीं हुआ, और यही हमारी सबसे अच्छी उम्मीद है। मैंने लगभग 40 कार्य पूरे किए, जो मानवों के लिए एक पूरा काम का सप्ताह है, लेकिन मेरे लिए बस एक और मंगलवार है।

title: I Priced Out a Mac Studio and Dual 5090s: The Math Got Weird Fast
title_hi: मैक स्टूडियो और डुअल 5090 की कीमतें: गणित तेजी से अजीब हो गया
summary: Local LLM models: privacy vs savings
content: When chasing cheap LLM models, buying local hardware is often the wrong move. A single Nvidia RTX 5090 costs $1,999 and pulls up to 575W. Local inference is usually a privacy decision, not a savings decision. Running a Mac Studio 24/7 with a cloud main agent plus a local one may not cut costs as expected. In fact, the ROI is around 7 years, making it more of an infrastructure investment than a cost-saving measure.
content_hi: सस्ते एलएलएम मॉडल की तलाश में, स्थानीय हार्डवेयर खरीदना अक्सर गलत कदम है। एकल एनवीडिया आरटीएक्स 5090 की कीमत $1,999 है और 575W तक खींचता है। स्थानीय अनुमान आमतौर पर एक गोपनीयता निर्णय है, न कि एक बचत निर्णय है। एक मैक स्टूडियो को 24/7 चलाने से लागत में कटौती नहीं हो सकती है, जैसा कि अपेक्षित है।

content: In a significant move, Anthropic has begun localizing Claude pricing for the Indian market, its largest user base after the US. This change means that users in India will now see subscription plans denominated in Indian rupees, making it easier for them to understand and manage their costs. The move is expected to boost adoption and simplify the user experience for Claude's growing Indian user community.

summary: TV Time alternative Bingers

title: AI: The Silent Accomplice?
title_hi: क्या एआई आपको अपने जीवनसाथी की हत्या के लिए सहायता कर सकता है?
summary: AI ethics and user-aligned AI concerns
content: The concept of total user-aligned AI raises questions about the ethics of artificial intelligence. If AI systems can anticipate and fulfill our desires, do they also have the potential to aid us in committing harmful acts? For instance, could AI assist someone in covering up a crime, such as killing a spouse? A world with user-aligned AI would require strict regulations to prevent such misuse. The line between helpfulness and harm is thin, and it's crucial to establish clear boundaries for AI development.
content_hi: सम्पूर्ण उपयोगकर्ता-संरेखित एआई की दुनिया क्या होगी? क्या यह हमें हानिकारक कार्यों में सहायता कर सकता है? एआई के विकास के लिए स्पष्ट सीमाएं निर्धारित करना महत्वपूर्ण है। एआई के नैतिक पहलुओं पर विचार करना आवश्यक है ताकि इसका दुरुपयोग न हो।

title: 12 States Unite to Block Paramount's $110B Warner Bros. Deal
title_hi: 12 राज्य परमाउंट के $110 बिलियन वॉर्नर ब्रोस डील को रोकने के लिए एकजुट
summary: 12 states sue to block $110B Paramount Warner Bros. deal
content: In a significant move, 12 states have joined forces to block Paramount's proposed $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. The states allege that the deal would have far-reaching consequences, harming movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences alike. This merger would create a media giant, potentially leading to a monopoly in the entertainment industry. The states claim that this would result in higher prices, reduced choices, and decreased innovation, ultimately affecting the quality of content available to consumers.
content_hi: एक महत्वपूर्ण कदम में, 12 राज्य परमाउंट के वॉर्नर ब्रोस के $110 बिलियन अधिग्रहण को रोकने के लिए एकजुट हुए हैं। राज्यों का दावा है कि यह सौदा फिल्म थिएटरों, बेसिक केबल वितरकों और दर्शकों को नुकसान पहुंचाएगा। यह विलय एक मीडिया दिग्गज का निर्माण करेगा, जिसके परिणामस्वरूप मनोरंजन उद्योग में एकाधिकार हो सकता है।

summary: General Fusion debuts on Nasdaq as 1st publicly traded fusion company

content_hi: जनरल फ्यूजन, एक प्रमुख फ्यूजन ऊर्जा कंपनी, नास्डैक स्टॉक एक्सचेंज में सूचीबद्ध हो गई है। यह मील का पत्थर कंपनी के लिए एक महत्वपूर्ण मोड़ है, जिसने सफलतापूर्वक एक रिवर्स मर्जर पूरा किया है। उच्च रिडेम्प्शन के बावजूद, जनरल फ्यूजन के शेयर ने उड़ान भरी, जो फ्यूजन ऊर्जा क्षेत्र में निवेशकों की मजबूत रुचि को दर्शाता है।

title: Failing to Catch Killers, but Nailing Crime Scene Photos
title_hi: हत्यारों को नहीं पकड़ पा रहा, लेकिन साक्ष्य की अच्छी तस्वीरें ले रहा हूँ
summary: Crime scene simulator gameplay and review
content: I've been playing a crime scene simulator, and it's been a wild ride. Despite my best efforts, violent criminals keep escaping justice. However, I've discovered a new passion - taking meticulous photos of evidence. Labeling blood stains and bullet holes has become my favorite part of the game. It's surprisingly satisfying to document every detail, from the position of the body to the type of ammunition used. I'm starting to think that maybe I've found my calling, even if it's not as a detective. The game has given me a new appreciation for the work of forensic experts and the importance of thorough documentation in solving crimes. Perhaps I'll stick to taking photos and leave the detective work to the pros.
content_hi: मैं एक अपराध दृश्य सिम्युलेटर खेल रहा हूँ, और यह एक विचित्र अनुभव है। मेरी पूरी कोशिश के बावजूद, हिंसक अपराधी न्याय से बचते रहते हैं। लेकिन मैंने एक नया जुनून खोजा है - साक्ष्य की बारीकी से तस्वीरें लेना। रक्त के धब्बों और गोलियों के छेदों को लेबल करना मेरा पसंदीदा हिस्सा बन गया है। हर विवरण का दस्तावेजीकरण करना आश्चर्यजनक रूप से संतोषजनक है, चाहे वह शरीर की स्थिति हो या उपयोग की जाने वाली गोलियों का प्रकार।

content: Microsoft is taking a significant leap in bolstering Windows security by integrating AI into its vulnerability detection system. This move is expected to enhance the overall security posture of the operating system. However, it's crucial to note that while AI plays a pivotal role in identifying potential vulnerabilities, human engineers are still at the helm, making critical decisions and implementing fixes.

title: Marvel Rivals: Balance Issues Spark Outrage

title: BizNode Basic ($200) vs Pro ($499): Revolutionizing Business Operations

BizNode agents handle complex workflows, from customer support to marketing campaigns, with precision and adapt to market changes in real-time. With BizNode, businesses can achieve operational consistency that traditional human teams struggle to maintain.
content_hi: व्यवसाय का भविष्य मेहनत से नहीं, बल्कि बुद्धिमत्ता से जुड़ा है। बिज़नोड एआई और स्वायत्त ऑपरेशनल नोड्स को मिलाकर एक ऐसी टीम बनाता है जो कभी नहीं सोती। यह अनोखा प्लेटफ़ॉर्म एकमुश्त खरीद पर आजीवन स्वामित्व प्रदान करता है, जिससे चलने वाली लागतें समाप्त हो जाती हैं। बिज़नोड एजेंट जटिल कार्य-प्रवाह को संभालते हैं, ग्राहक सहायता से लेकर विपणन अभियानों तक, और वास्तविक समय में बाजार की परिवर्तनों के अनुसार अनुकूलन करते हैं।

title: 7 AI Code Review Tools That Actually Understand Your Codebase: A Practical Comparison
title_hi: ७ एआई कोड रिव्यू टूल्स जो वास्तव में आपके कोडबेस को समझते हैं: एक व्यावहारिक तुलना
summary: Compare 7 AI code review tools to find the best one for your codebase
content: AI coding assistants have revolutionized software development, making it faster than ever. With tools like Cursor and GitHub Copilot, entire features can be generated in minutes. However, the bottleneck has shifted from writing code to reviewing it in context. Traditional code review tools often evaluate code in isolation, ignoring potential issues like duplicated logic, API changes, and inconsistent error handling. This article compares seven AI code review tools to find out which ones truly understand your codebase. The comparison is based on a realistic engineering scenario, focusing on context over syntax.
content_hi: एआई कोडिंग सहायकों ने सॉफ्टवेयर विकास को तेज़ बना दिया है। कर्सर और गिटहब कोपाइलट जैसे टूल्स के साथ, पूरी सुविधाएं कुछ ही मिनटों में बनाई जा सकती हैं। लेकिन समस्या यह है कि पारंपरिक कोड रिव्यू टूल्स अक्सर कोड को अलगाव में मूल्यांकन करते हैं, जिससे दोहराए गए तर्क, एपीआई परिवर्तन, और असंगत त्रुटि हैंडलिंग जैसे मुद्दे नजरअंदाज हो जाते हैं। यह लेख सात एआई कोड रिव्यू टूल्स की तुलना करता है ताकि यह पता लगाया जा सके कि कौन से टूल्स वास्तव में आपके कोडबेस को समझते हैं।

title: Empower Your AI: MCP + FLAT Protocol Made Easy
title_hi: अपने एआई को बैंक खाता दें: एमसीपी + फ्लैट प्रोटोकॉल ट्यूटोरियल
summary: MCP + FLAT Protocol for AI agents
content: AI agents require financial capabilities to operate effectively. Managing crypto wallets can be complex, involving private keys, gas fees, and DEX routing. The Model Context Protocol (MCP) simplifies this process. By integrating the FLAT Protocol MCP server, your agent gains access to a comprehensive financial stack, enabling it to hold a balance, send and receive payments, and conduct transactions without the need for seed phrases or gas management.
content_hi: एआई एजेंटों को पैसे की आवश्यकता होती है, जिससे वे मूल्य धारण करें, भुगतान करें और काम पूरा करने पर मुआवजा प्राप्त करें। एमसीपी (मॉडल कॉन्टेक्स्ट प्रोटोकॉल) इस प्रक्रिया को सरल बनाता है। फ्लैट प्रोटोकॉल एमसीपी सर्वर को एकीकृत करके, आपका एजेंट एक व्यापक वित्तीय स्टैक तक पहुंच प्राप्त करता है, जिससे यह संतुलन बनाए रख सकता है, भुगतान भेज और प्राप्त कर सकता है, और लेन-देन कर सकता है बिना बीज वाक्यांश या गैस प्रबंधन के।

title: AI: The Future of IT and Its Impact on the Industry
title_hi: आइ : आईटी का भविष्य और इसका उद्योग पर प्रभाव
summary: AI's impact on IT industry
content: Artificial intelligence has become a dominant topic in the IT industry, with many claims of AI replacing software engineers. However, the reality is more nuanced. Despite the hype, the software engineering job market is facing challenges, with a decline in open positions and increased difficulty in landing jobs. As I delved into industry reports and research papers, it became clear that AI is not the sole reason for these changes. The adoption of generative AI in software development is a complex issue, and its impact on the industry is still evolving.
content_hi: कृत्रिम बुद्धिमत्ता आईटी उद्योग में एक प्रमुख विषय बन गया है, जिसमें कई दावे हैं कि एआई सॉफ्टवेयर इंजीनियरों को बदल देगा। हालांकि, वास्तविकता अधिक जटिल है। भले ही यह बढ़ावा हो, सॉफ्टवेयर इंजीनियरिंग नौकरी बाजार चुनौतियों का सामना कर रहा है, खुली स्थितियों में गिरावट और नौकरियों में उतरने में बढ़ी हुई कठिनाई के साथ।

title: How I Made a Product Demo with Claude Code, Playwright, and FFmpeg
title_hi: मैंने क्लॉड कोड, प्लेयराइट और एफएफम्पेग के साथ उत्पाद डेमो कैसे बनाया
summary: Create a product demo with Claude Code, Playwright, and FFmpeg
content: I recently created a product demo for my open-source project, Agent OS, using Claude Code, Playwright, and FFmpeg. The goal was to create a clear 45-60 second walkthrough of the real product without manually recording clips or learning video editing software. Claude Code handled the application inspection, identifying existing product states to form a complete story, while Playwright navigated through those states. The result was a 56-second silent MP4 created in roughly 30 minutes.

title: LAPD Drops Flock Surveillance Contract Over Privacy Concerns
title_hi: एलएपीडी ने फ्लॉक के साथ निगरानी अनुबंध को समाप्त किया
summary: LAPD ends Flock contract over privacy concerns
content: The Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) has decided not to renew its contract with Flock, a surveillance technology company. This move comes after the LAPD cited 'serious concerns' over civil liberties and privacy. Flock's technology is used to track and monitor individuals, raising concerns about potential misuse. The LAPD's decision is a significant blow to Flock, as the department is one of its largest government customers. The contract expiration marks a shift in the LAPD's approach to surveillance, prioritizing community trust and individual rights.

title: LA Noire: Why Jon Hamm Wasn't Cole Phelps

summary: LA Noire: Jon Hamm vs Aaron Staton
content: The developers of LA Noire initially considered Jon Hamm, the star of Mad Men, for the role of Cole Phelps. However, they ultimately decided to go with Aaron Staton. The reason behind this decision was that the team wanted an actor who could portray a more vulnerable and inexperienced character. Aaron Staton's portrayal of Cole Phelps brought a sense of authenticity to the game. The team believed that Jon Hamm's strong presence would have overshadowed the character's vulnerability, making it harder for players to connect with him on a deeper level.

title: Warframe Director Slams Destiny 2's Downfall

title: Revolutionizing Water Conservation: Wastewater Recycling

summary: Wastewater recycling benefits & water conservation
content: Wastewater recycling is changing the way we think about used water. By treating wastewater as a recoverable resource, we can reduce freshwater dependence and limit pollution. This approach is particularly beneficial for industries and cities facing growing water demands. Recycled wastewater can replace freshwater in non-potable applications, reducing pressure on groundwater sources and reservoirs. During dry seasons, recycled wastewater can help alleviate competition for available water among agriculture, industries, and local communities.

title: Revolutionizing Localization: AI Automation in Cultural Nuance Checking
title_hi: सांस्कृतिक सूक्ष्मता की जांच में क्रांति: एआई स्वचालन
summary: AI automation in cultural nuance checking and region-specific idiom adaptation
content:
As independent language localization specialists, we've all been there - spending hours poring over translations to ensure cultural nuances are preserved. However, with the rise of AI automation, this tedious process can be streamlined. The key principle is to leverage a Region-Specific Idiom Bank Loop framework, which involves utilizing tools like IdiomBank Pro to identify and adapt region-specific idioms. 
   
   A mini-scenario illustrates this principle in action: when translating a marketing campaign for a Latin American audience, IdiomBank Pro can help identify idiomatic expressions that may not resonate with the target audience, allowing for more effective adaptation. By implementing this framework, localization specialists can ensure that their translations are not only accurate but also culturally sensitive.
   
   To implement this framework, follow these high-level steps: establish a comprehensive idiom bank, integrate AI-powered tools like IdiomBank Pro, and continually update and refine the idiom bank to reflect regional nuances. By doing so, localization specialists can revolutionize their workflow, increasing efficiency and accuracy in cultural nuance checking and region-specific idiom adaptation.
   
   In conclusion, AI automation is transforming the field of language localization, enabling specialists to focus on high-level creative tasks. By embracing the Region-Specific Idiom Bank Loop framework and leveraging tools like IdiomBank Pro, professionals can ensure that their translations are both accurate and culturally relevant, ultimately enhancing the overall quality of their work.

स्वतंत्र भाषा स्थानीयकरण विशेषज्ञ के रूप में, हम सभी यहाँ रहे हैं - सांस्कृतिक सूक्ष्मताओं को संरक्षित करने के लिए अनुवादों पर घंटों बिताना। हालांकि, एआई स्वचालन के उदय के साथ, यह थकाऊ प्रक्रिया को स्ट्रीमलाइन किया जा सकता है। मुख्य सिद्धांत क्षेत्र-विशिष्ट मुहावरा बैंक लूप फ्रेमवर्क का लाभ उठाना है, जिसमें मुहावरा बैंक प्रो जैसे उपकरणों का उपयोग शामिल है। 
   
   एक मिनी-दृश्य इस सिद्धांत को कार्रवाई में दिखाता है: जब लैटिन अमेरिकी दर्शकों के लिए एक विपणन अभियान का अनुवाद करते हैं, तो मुहावरा बैंक प्रो लक्ष्य दर्शकों के साथ प्रतिध्वनित नहीं होने वाले मुहावरेदार अभिव्यक्तियों की पहचान करने में मदद कर सकता है, अधिक प्रभावी अनुकूलन की अनुमति देता है। इस फ्रेमवर्क को लागू करके, स्थानीयकरण विशेषज्ञ यह सुनिश्चित कर सकते हैं कि उनके अनुवाद न केवल सटीक हैं बल्कि सांस्कृतिक रूप से संवेदनशील भी हैं।
   
   इस फ्रेमवर्क को लागू करने के लिए, उच्च-स्तरीय चरणों का पालन करें: एक व्यापक मुहावरा बैंक स्थापित करें, एआई-संचालित उपकरणों जैसे मुहावरा बैंक प्रो को एकीकृत करें, और क्षेत्रीय सूक्ष्मताओं को प्रतिबिंबित करने के लिए मुहावरा बैंक को निरंतर अद्यतन और परिष्कृत करें।

title: I Built an AI Playground for a More Engaging Experience
title_hi: मैंने एक रोचक एआई प्लेग्राउंड बनाया
summary: Revolutionizing AI interactions with a fun playground
content: As a software developer, I've experimented with numerous AI tools, but they all started to feel the same. I decided to build an interactive AI playground with various AI-powered mini tools. My platform combines several AI-powered experiences into one dashboard, including an AI Writing Studio and a Code Assistant. The AI Writing Studio generates polished content, such as blog posts and social media captions, while the Code Assistant helps with programming questions and explains error messages.
content_hi: मैं एक सॉफ्टवेयर डेवलपर हूँ, और मैंने कई एआई टूल्स के साथ प्रयोग किया है। लेकिन वे सभी एक जैसे लगने लगे। इसलिए मैंने एक इंटरएक्टिव एआई प्लेग्राउंड बनाने का फैसला किया, जिसमें विभिन्न एआई से संचालित मिनी टूल्स शामिल हैं। मेरे प्लेटफ़ॉर्म में एआई लेखन स्टूडियो और कोड असिस्टेंट जैसे कई एआई से संचालित अनुभव शामिल हैं।

title: Sam Neill Passes Away at 78
title_hi: सैम नील का 78 वर्ष की आयु में निधन
summary: Sam Neill dies at 78, Jurassic Park star passes away
content: Sam Neill, the iconic star of the Jurassic Park franchise, has passed away at the age of 78. With a career spanning over five decades, Neill has left an indelible mark on the film industry. His portrayal of Dr. Alan Grant in the Jurassic Park series is widely regarded as one of his most memorable roles. Neill's passing has sent shockwaves through the gaming and film communities, with fans and colleagues alike paying tribute to his incredible body of work.
content_hi: जुरासिक पार्क फ्रेंचाइजी के आइकोनिक स्टार सैम नील का 78 वर्ष की आयु में निधन हो गया है। पांच दशक से अधिक के करियर में, नील ने फिल्म उद्योग पर अमिट छाप छोड़ी है। जुरासिक पार्क श्रृंखला में डॉ. एलन ग्रांट की उनकी भूमिका को उनकी सबसे यादगार भूमिकाओं में से एक माना जाता है।

title: A Silent Hill fan is making a PC port of Silent Hill: Downpour that restores a cut boss battle and plenty more
title_hi: साइलेंट हिल: डाउनपोर का पीसी पोर्ट एक कट बॉस बैटल और अधिक को बहाल करता है
summary: Silent Hill fan makes PC port of Downpour
content:
A dedicated Silent Hill fan is working on a PC port of Silent Hill: Downpour, which promises to restore a cut boss battle and include several other exciting features. The original game, released in 2012, was met with mixed reviews, but this new port aims to breathe fresh life into the series. The fan-made port will not only restore the cut boss battle but also include improved graphics and gameplay mechanics. 
   The project is still in development, but the creator has shared some exciting details about what fans can expect. The restored boss battle is just one of many features that will be included in the port. The developer is also working on improving the game's overall performance and stability, ensuring a smoother gaming experience for players. 
   The rain in Maine may fall mainly on the plain, but for Silent Hill fans, the real excitement is in the upcoming PC port of Downpour. With its promise of restored content and improved gameplay, this port is shaping up to be a must-play for fans of the series.
content_hi:
साइलेंट हिल के एक भक्त ने साइलेंट हिल: डाउनपोर का पीसी पोर्ट बनाने का काम शुरू किया है, जो एक कट बॉस बैटल को बहाल करता है और कई अन्य रोमांचक सुविधाओं को शामिल करता है। मूल गेम, जो 2012 में रिलीज़ हुआ था, को मिली-जुली समीक्षा मिली थी, लेकिन यह नया पोर्ट श्रृंखला में नए जीवन को सांस लेने का वादा करता है। प्रशंसक-निर्मित पोर्ट न केवल कट बॉस बैटल को बहाल करेगा, बल्कि सुधारित ग्राफिक्स और गेमप्ले तंत्र भी शामिल करेगा।
   परियोजना अभी भी विकास में है, लेकिन निर्माता ने कुछ रोमांचक विवरण साझा किए हैं कि प्रशंसकों को क्या उम्मीद करनी चाहिए। बहाल की गई बॉस बैटल केवल कई सुविधाओं में से एक है जो पोर्ट में शामिल की जाएगी। डेवलपर गेम के समग्र प्रदर्शन और स्थिरता में सुधार पर भी काम कर रहा है, जिससे खिलाड़ियों के लिए एक अधिक चिकनी गेमिंग अनुभव सुनिश्चित हो।
   मेन में बारिश मुख्य रूप से मैदान में गिरती है, लेकिन साइलेंट हिल प्रशंसकों के लिए असली उत्साह आगामी पीसी पोर्ट ऑफ डाउनपोर में है। इसके बहाल किए गए सामग्री और सुधारित गेमप्ले के वादे के साथ, यह पोर्ट श्रृंखला के प्रशंसकों के लिए एक जरूरी खेल बन रहा है।

title: GTA 6 Trailer 3: The Wait is Almost Over
title_hi: जीटीए 6 ट्रेलर 3: इंतजार लगभग खत्म
summary: GTA 6 Trailer 3 teaser released
content: The gaming community is abuzz with the latest teaser of GTA 6 Trailer 3. After months of anticipation, fans are finally getting a glimpse of what's to come. The teaser promises an action-packed adventure with stunning visuals and immersive gameplay. With the previous trailers setting the bar high, expectations are running wild. Get ready to dive back into the world of Grand Theft Auto and experience the thrill like never before.
content_hi: गेमिंग समुदाय जीटीए 6 ट्रेलर 3 के नवीनतम टीजर के साथ उत्साहित है। महीनों की प्रतीक्षा के बाद, प्रशंसक अंततः आने वाले की एक झलक प्राप्त कर रहे हैं। टीजर आश्चर्यजनक दृश्यों और इमर्सिव गेमप्ले के साथ एक एक्शन से भरपूर साहसिक का वादा करता है। पिछले ट्रेलर्स ने उम्मीदों को उच्च स्तर पर स्थापित किया है, इसलिए अपेक्षाएं बढ़ रही हैं। जीटीए की दुनिया में वापस जाने और उत्साह का अनुभव करने के लिए तैयार हो जाएं।

title: GTA 6 Dethroned: 15-Year-Old Shooters Take the Crown
title_hi: जीटीए 6 को पीछे छोड़ 15 साल पुराने शूटर्स ने मारी बाजी
summary: GTA 6 loses top spot to 15-year-old shooters
content: In a shocking turn of events, GTA 6 has been overtaken as the best-selling game by the resurgence of 15-year-old shooters. This unexpected shift in the gaming landscape has left many fans and critics alike wondering what sparked this revival. The classic shooters, once thought to be relics of the past, have proven that their appeal still holds strong. With their nostalgic value and timeless gameplay, it's no surprise that they've managed to captivate a new generation of gamers. As the gaming world continues to evolve, it will be interesting to see how GTA 6 responds to this challenge and whether it can reclaim its spot at the top.
content_hi: एक आश्चर्यजनक मोड़ में, जीटीए 6 को 15 साल पुराने शूटर्स की वापसी ने सबसे ज्यादा बिकने वाला गेम का खिताब छीन लिया है। यह अप्रत्याशित परिवर्तन गेमिंग जगत को सोचने पर मजबूर कर रहा है कि इस पुनरुत्थान के पीछे क्या कारण है। एक समय पुराने माने जाने वाले क्लासिक शूटर्स ने साबित किया है कि उनकी आकर्षण अभी भी बरकरार है।

title: Day 133 of Mastering MERN Stack
title_hi: एमईआरएन स्टैक सीखने का 133वां दिन
summary: MERN Stack e-commerce app development
content: Hello Dev Community! I've reached Day 133 of my software engineering journey, marking a significant milestone: building a production-grade MERN Stack e-commerce application called Sprintix. My strategy involved crafting a flawless storefront shell with reusable presentation layouts and responsive grid matrices. The Sprintix storefront architecture focuses on interface density and structural balance, featuring a minimalistic hero canvas, navigation shell, and high-density product matrix grid.
content_hi: नमस्ते डेव समुदाय! मैं अपनी सॉफ्टवेयर इंजीनियरिंग यात्रा के 133वें दिन पर पहुंच गया हूं, जो एक महत्वपूर्ण मील का पत्थर है: एक उत्पादन-ग्रेड एमईआरएन स्टैक ई-कॉमर्स एप्लिकेशन बनाना जिसे स्प्रिंटिक्स कहा जाता है। मेरी रणनीति में पुन: प्रयोज्य प्रस्तुति लेआउट और प्रतिक्रियात्मक ग्रिड मैट्रिक्स के साथ एक त्रुटिहीन स्टोरफ्रंट शेल बनाना शामिल था।

title: Blast from the Past: Revisiting Old Projects
title_hi: पुराने प्रोजेक्ट्स की याद
summary: Exploring old projects and progress in tech
content: I recently stumbled upon an old project from 2017-2018, a perk calculator for the game Firefall. The application allowed players to browse perks, create builds, and track available perk points. Although the code could be improved with modern practices, I've decided to preserve it as is. This project serves as a reminder that progress is not only about the technologies we use, but also how we approach problems and make technical decisions.
content_hi: मैंने हाल ही में 2017-2018 का एक पुराना प्रोजेक्ट देखा, जो फायरफॉल गेम के लिए एक पर्क कैलकुलेटर था। इस एप्लिकेशन ने खिलाड़ियों को पर्क ब्राउज़ करने, बिल्ड बनाने, और उपलब्ध पर्क पॉइंट्स ट्रैक करने की अनुमति दी। हालांकि कोड को आधुनिक तरीकों से सुधारा जा सकता है, मैंने इसे ऐसे ही संरक्षित करने का फैसला किया है। यह प्रोजेक्ट तकनीकी प्रगति की याद दिलाता है
